data.title: Capgemini Extends AI Partnership with Peugeot to Boost Hypercar Performance, Cut Emissions
data.description:
Capgemini said Thursday it extended its partnership with Peugeot Sport to boost the 9X8 Hypercar performance using artificial intelligence and cut carbon emissions.

The partnership will leverage generative AI data to make real-time decisions to enhance the Hypercar's performance competing in the FIA World Endurance Championship, the French information technology company said.

The company will also support Peugeot's decarbonization efforts by implementing 30 measures to lower greenhouse gas emissions by 2030.

CapitaLand Ascendas REIT FY24 DPU 15.205 Singapore Cents Vs. 15.160 Singapore Cents >A17U.SG
A pre-engineering starts at the Husum mill integrate to change the production of BM2
data.description:
Metsä Board Oyj

Metsä Board Corporation Investor News 6 February 2025

Metsä Board, part of Metsä Group, announced in March 2024 that it will start a development programme to produce new products on the current uncoated white kraftliner production line (BM2) at its Husum mill integrate in Sweden. The aim was to find alternatives for uncoated kraftliner production.

As a result, Metsä Tissue, also part of Metsä Group, in cooperation with Metsä Board will start a pre-engineering project to investigate potential change of product produced on Husum BM2. The planned investment would enable new capacity of approximately 80,000 tonnes of foodservice packaging papers and siliconised greaseproof papers. The pre-engineering phase is expected to take 12 months and the investment decision could be made earliest in the first quarter of 2026. The start-up of the rebuilt line would be earliest in Q4/2027.

The production of white kraftliner will continue at full capacity at least until the possible investment shutdown of BM2.

Metsä Board Husum mill in Sweden is an integrated board and pulp mill producing folding boxboard, uncoated white kraftliner and bleached chemical pulp.

Photo: https://databank.metsagroup.com/l/C6KzmcRgMjnW

METSÄ BOARD CORPORATION

Further information:

Henri Sederholm, CFO, tel. +358 10 465 4913

Katri Sundström, VP, Investor relations, tel. +358 10 462 0101  

Metsä Board 

metsagroup.com/metsaboard

Metsä Board is a producer of lightweight and high-quality folding boxboards, food service boards and white kraftliners. The pure fresh fibres we use in our products are a safe, renewable and recyclable resource, that can be traced back to northern forests. We aim to have completely fossil-free mills and raw materials by the end of 2030. We promote a culture of diversity, equality and inclusion.

Metsä Board is listed on the Nasdaq Helsinki. In 2023 our sales totalled EUR 1.9 billion, and we have around 2,300 employees. Metsä Board is part of Metsä Group, whose parent company Metsäliitto Cooperative is owned by over 90,000 Finnish forest owners. The sales of the whole Metsä Group were EUR 6.1 billion.

Follow Metsä Board: LinkedIn  X  YouTube  Instagram

data.title: First Abu Dhabi Bank falls on slowing profit growth in Q4
data.description:
** Shares in First Abu Dhabi Bank fall more than 3.3% to 13.9 dirhams

** UAE's biggest lender reports net profit of 4.2 bln dirhams ($1.14 billion) in Q4, up 4% YoY

** However, this was a sharp decline in growth from Q3, when profits rose 46%

** Stock set for biggest one-day fall since August

** Eight brokerages rate the stock "buy" or higher, five "hold" and one "sell"; median PT is 15.35 dirhams - LSEG data

($1 = 3.6727 UAE dirham)

data.title: Here’s Why Cardano (ADA) Could Experience a ‘Monster Parabolic Rally’ (Analyst)
data.description:
Key points:
  • Analysts suggest Cardano (ADA) could be at the start of a major resurgence, with predictions of a potential surge to double-digit territory, citing strong fundamentals and past bull cycle performance.
  • On the other hand, large investors recently dumped over 330 million ADA tokens, increasing market supply and potentially triggering further price declines in the short term.

TL;DR

$15 Per Coin?

Cardano’s ADA was among the top-performing cryptocurrencies toward the end of 2024, with its price rising to an almost three-year high of approximately $1.30.

Since then, though, it has lost some steam, and its valuation briefly tanked to as low as $0.60 duringthe crypto crashon February 3. The bulls managed to reclaim some of the lost ground in the following days, and ADA currently trades at around $0.75 (a 30% decline on a monthly scale).

Despite the recent downtrend, popular industry participants believe the asset remains poised for a massive surge. X user Ali Martinez reminded that ADA’s price exploded from less than $0.1 to over $3 during the previous bull cycle in mid-2021. He assumed that the token could be “at the very beginning of a monster parabolic rally,” and if history repeats, the valuation could skyrocket to $15 in the near future.

CryptoRus supported the thesis, suggesting that ADA “is looking like it could be primed to pump.” Lucky told their over two million followers on X that “strong fundamentals and rising adoption make Cardano a solid bet before the next big move.”

“The ecosystem keeps growing, reinforcing its market position,” the analyst added.The Bears Might Continue to Prevail

Despite the optimism from multiple market observers, some important factors suggest that ADA bulls might experience more pain in the short term.

As CryptoPotato reported earlier this week, whales have offloaded more than 330 million tokens (worth almost $250 million at the time).

A significant sell-off by large investors triggers a surplus of supply into the market, potentially exceeding demand and driving prices lower. Moreover, the actions of these whales could spark panic among smaller traders, further intensifying the downward pressure on the asset’s value.

Conversely, when large investors accumulate a substantial amount of ADA, it can have the opposite impact. As reported by CryptoPotato, whales bought over 130 million tokens in late November when the price was around $1. Shortly after, ADA surged to its local peak of $1.30.

data.title: Pernod Ricard CEO: Significant Decline in Gifting Led to Weak Chinese New Year
Slovenia's CPI rose to 2.0% y/y in January
data.description:
Slovenia's annual consumer price inflation came in at 2.0% in January, according to data released by the Statistical Office (SURS) on Thursday, up from 1.9% in December.

Group transport prices, which rose by 3.0%, and food and non-alcoholic beverage prices, which increased by 2.3%, were the largest contributors to the annual growth rate, with prices in restaurant and hotel prices also up by 4.8%, SURS said.

data.title: Zhejiang Jiuzhou Pharmaceutical's Zhejiang Base Passes US FDA Inspection
data.description:
Zhejiang Jiuzhou Pharmaceutical subsidiary Zhejiang Ruibo passed an on-site Current Good Manufacturing Practice inspection conducted by the US Food and Drug Administration.

The inspection covered quality systems, materials, production, packaging, labeling, equipment, and laboratory controls, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The inspection site was the active pharmaceutical ingredient base at 18 Nanyang 3rd Road, Linhai Park in China's Zhejiang province.

Shares of the drugmaker closed 1% higher Thursday.

data.title: India's Emcure Pharma gains on Q3 profit jump
data.description:
** Shares of Emcure Pharma climb 3% to 1,287.50 rupees

** Drugmaker reports 35.5% Y/Y growth in Q3 consol net profit; rev from ops up ~18% Y/Y

** Its international business, biggest contributor to rev, jumped 23% Y/Y, while domestic, second-biggest, rose 12% Y/Y

** Stock, however, down ~5% since trading debut in July 2024

data.title: India's 2024 tea output drops 7.8%, lifts average prices
data.description:
India's tea production in 2024 fell 7.8% from a year earlier to 1284.78 million kg, on lower output in the top-producing north-eastern state of Assam, the state-run Tea Board said in a statement.

Production in Assam, which accounts for more than half the country's output, fell to 649.84 million kg in 2024, down from the previous year's 688.33 million kg, as heatwaves and floods during the peak harvesting season slashed output, the board said.

India, the world's second-biggest tea producer, exports CTC (crush-tear-curl) grade mainly to Egypt and the United Kingdom, with the orthodox variety shipped to Iraq, Iran and Russia.

The production shortfall has lifted prices significantly, with average tea prices in 2024 at 198.76 rupees per kg, a jump of nearly 18% from the previous year.

data.title: State Bank of India's Consolidated Profit Jumps in Fiscal Q3
data.description:
State Bank of India consolidated net profit jumped to 188.5 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal third quarter from 110.64 billion rupees a year ago.

Earnings per share in the quarter ended Dec. 31, 2024, climbed to 21.12 rupees from 12.40 rupees a year earlier, according to an Indian bourse filing on Thursday.

Interest earned by the lender in fiscal Q3 increased to 1.25 trillion rupees from 1.13 trillion rupees a year earlier.

data.title: Anglo American Warns of De Beers Impairment Amid Subdued Diamond Demand — Update
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Anglo American expects its De Beers diamond business to book an impairment as the mining giant reviews the value of the unit it hopes to offload.

The London-listed miner said Thursday that the review would examine De Beers's value in light of continued subdued demand for diamonds as it seeks to exit the business. Last year, Anglo wrote down the book value of De Beers by $1.6 billion to $7.6 billion.

After a failed takeover approach by rival BHP last year, Anglo has simplified its business to focus on copper, premium iron ore and crop nutrients. It has so far disposed of its steelmaking coal business and is the process of offloading its shares in Anglo American Platinum before an expected demerger by the middle of this year. While the company said Thursday that it is making progress in exiting its nickel business, the diamond business is proving harder to dispose of.

Demand for De Beers's diamonds has fallen significantly as cheaper manufactured gemstones have risen in popularity and global economic growth has remained weak. While the decline in demand has been universal, Anglo on Thursday highlighted waning Chinese sales.

The company said it expects De Beers's full-year earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization to be marginally negative in 2024. The division booked $72 million in underlying Ebitda in 2023.

While the company said Thursday that it continues to prepare the business for separation, its timeline for selling continues to slip. The division has the potential to be a significant cash drag on the miner for the next couple of years, RBC Capital Markets analysts wrote last month.

De Beers and its partners produce around one-third of the world's rough diamonds by value, according to its website. Anglo owns 85% of De Beers, with the remaining 15% owned by the government of Botswana.

Anglo on Thursday cut its 2025 diamond production guidance to between 20 million to 23 million carats from previous expectations of 30 million to 33 million carats

In its production update, Anglo said fourth-quarter diamond output fell 26% on year to 5.8 million carats as it cut production in response to low demand and higher-than-normal levels of inventory.

In early morning European trade, shares were trading up 3.8% at 2,420 pence. They are currently up 2.4% in the year to date and 3.1% higher over the month.

Anglo said Thursday that it dug out 198,000 tons of copper, 14% less than in the fourth quarter of 2023, due to the planned shutdown of its smaller Los Bronces plant.

Meanwhile, across the business, all divisions met their full-year production guidance, it said.

The company kept its full-year guidance for copper production for 2025 and 2026 unchanged.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Pernod Ricard CEO Is Confident in Strategy, Operating Model to Tackle Challenges
Itochu Considering Investment in 7-Eleven Owner — Update
data.description:
By Kosaku Narioka

Japanese trading house Itochu is considering investing in the owner of 7-Eleven, emerging as the latest player in an international takeover battle over one of the world's biggest convenience-store operators.

Itochu Chief Financial Officer Tsuyoshi Hachimura said Thursday that the company is considering investing in Seven & i Holdings. An Itochu spokeswoman confirmed the comments he made at an earnings briefing earlier in the day.

The 7-Eleven owner in September rejected an initial $39 billion buyout bid from Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard, saying the proposal underestimated the company's value. Couche-Tard later raised its offer to about $47 billion.

In November, Seven & i said it had received a buyout proposal from Junro Ito, a top Seven & i executive and son of the founder of the Japanese retail giant, but didn't provide details.

Private-equity firm KKR was considering joining the Seven & i founding family to take the company private, though talks were in the very initial stages, The Wall Street Journal reported in January.

Itochu hasn't decided whether it would invest in Seven & i or how, the spokeswoman said Thursday.

Seven & i Chief Financial Officer Yoshimichi Maruyama said last month that the company was considering options, including Couche-Tard's bid, Ito's proposal and a standalone growth strategy.

Seven & i has said it will bring in strategic partners for its business of supermarkets, specialty stores and other operations and spin off and list the holding company.

The Japanese company has said it will continue to consider strategic initiatives to unlock its North American convenience-store business's potential value as well as optimal capital relations with its banking unit. The retail giant has said it will accelerate expansion to new regions to seek growth in markets worldwide.

Write to Kosaku Narioka at kosaku.narioka@wsj.com

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0938 GMT - Investors have underestimated the potential of Babcock International's nuclear division, which is the standout performer in the U.K. defense company's latest update, JP Morgan analysts David Perry and Lucy Fitzgerald say. The U.K. government's commitment to the British nuclear-powered submarine fleet underpins the future growth of this division, they add. Some investors have raised concerns about the ability of the U.K. government to boost defense spending, but these seem unfounded, the analysts say. They point to a recent U.K. contract won by Rolls-Royce--its largest ever U.K. defense contract--as well as Babcock reiterating views of much stronger earnings from the U.K. defense market. Shares are up 11.5% at 605 pence. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0938 GMT - Babcock International hasn't disclosed its contract backlog, but Panmure analysts Joe Brent and Sanjay Vidyarthi say they don't expect it to grow by much. The U.K. defense company may benefit from global calls for increased defense spending, but should be wary of demands for better value for the taxpayer, which may result in more intense pressure on pricing, they warn. The analysts keep their hold recommendation but increase their target price on the stock to 550 pence from 490 pence. Shares are down 0.2% at 5.43 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0932 GMT - Maersk's share buyback of up to $2 billion announced late Wednesday should provide optimism given recent uncertainties over the disruptions in the Red Sea, Barclays analysts say. Added to that, the Danish shipping company has set its targets for 2025, with Ebitda guidance of between $6 billion and $9 billion, compared with Barclays's estimates of $6.5 billion, they say in a note. Maersk's 2025 EBIT is also in line with expectations, and global container volume is guided to grow at around 4%, they add. However, the decision to launch the buyback with guidance in line means that Barclays is likely to use nearly all of its cash buffer for 2025, meaning there will be little to no cash for acquisitions if the buyback is completed, the analysts add. Shares are up 7.8% at 11,715 Danish kronor.(michael.susin@wsj.com)

0931 GMT - Petronas may face a lower financial impact than initially expected following a recent policy shift in Sarawak's gas sector, Citi analyst Steven Chan says in a note. Sarawak's state-owned Petroleum Sarawak will now manage only domestic gas supply, while LNG exports remain under Petronas's purview. Concerns had risen that Petronas's revenue and free cash flow could be affected as Petroleum Sarawak became the sole gas aggregator in 2H 2024. Chan notes that LNG exports account for 94% of Sarawak's natural gas output and 27% of Petronas's 2023 revenue. He views the development as positive, easing concerns over potential upstream capital expenditure cuts by Petronas and ensuring the Malaysian state-owned energy company retains exclusive control over exploration and distribution with no disruptions to existing agreements. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0930 GMT - A.P. Moeller-Maersk's investors are likely to focus on the timing of its return to the Suez Canal, J.P. Morgan analysts say in a note. This is especially so after several vessels of rival shipping company MSC were seen crossing through the Red Sea, the analysts say. Furthermore, the Danish firm's Ocean division seems to be heading for a loss in the second half based on management guidance for 2025, they say. EBIT is expected between $0 and $3 billion, and Ebitda between $6 billion and $9 billion, dependent on the timing of the Red Sea reopening, they say. (anthony.orunagoriainoff@dowjones.com)

0923 GMT - Babcock International's decision to raise its full-year guidance comes amid a strong performance of its nuclear and marine units, which grew 22% and 6% respectively, Jefferies analysts Chloe Lemarie and Ben Brown say. The nuclear division in particular is now expected to grow by double digits in 2025, with the civil-nuclear, submarine-support and infrastructure units performing better than expected, they write. The U.K. defense company remains one of Jefferies's preferred names and is trading close to its record discount to European defense peers, at 44%, the analysts note. Shares are down 0.2% at 5.43 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0922 GMT - The FTSE 100 index has hit a new intra-day record, boosted by AstraZeneca and Anglo American after both companies issued reports, and on expectations that the Bank of England will cut interest rates again later Thursday. The index hit a high of 8,721, with AstraZeneca shares up 4.8% and Anglo American shares trading 4.3% higher. Earlier AstraZeneca said that it expects further sales and earnings growth this year after beating market forecasts in the latest quarter, while Anglo American reported market-beating production figures for the same period. The BoE is expected to lower interest rates by 25 basis points from the current 4.75%, which was lowered by the same amount in November. (ian.walker@wsj.com)

0918 GMT - Babcock International should continue to benefit from healthy revenue growth opportunities soundly financed by its balance sheet, Shore Capital analysts Robin Speakman and Jamie Murray say. Global defense spending continues to rise and the trend to invest in nuclear, including decommissioning, supports Babcock's business, they write. The analysts expect the U.K. defense company to continue to post broadly positive results in 2026 and 2027, but caution that the group is yet to issue guidance on the impact that the raise to national insurance contributions announced by the U.K. government in its budget last October will have over the business. Shares are down 0.2% at 5.43 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

0909 GMT - Carlsberg reported a solid performance in 2024, with sales and EBIT broadly in line with expectations and delivering earnings ahead of market estimates, Citi analysts say. The Danish brewer posted surprising adjusted EPS for the year of 54.9 Danish kronor, around 5% ahead of consensus, they say in a note. Looking ahead, Carlsberg expects 2025 organic EBIT growth between 1% to 5% after including the 200bps to 300bps impact from the loss of San Miguel. "Overall, this is in line with our expectations and consensus estimates of around 3% but is slightly better than some were fearing ahead of the print," they say. EPS estimates in 2025 could be upgraded by a low single-digit percentage growth, the analysts add. Shares are up 2.1% at 858 Danish kronor. (michael.susin@wsj.com)

0909 GMT - China's retail data during Lunar New Year holiday is not enough to support a positive outlook, HSBC analysts Lina Yan and Yimin Wang in a research note. Retail sales grew 5% but the analysts don't read too much into the that as it could be merely a release of spending power after a weak 2024. Spending patterns matter more and they flag some trends that could persist in 2025. These include cost-conscious spending, more demand for certain products due to government stimulus, young consumers leading consumption. Rather than hoping for a turnaround in the consumption cycle, their top picks for 2025 include Giant Biogene for product strength; Anta for resilient earnings growth; Yum China for shareholder returns; and CR Beverage for value relative to profit growth. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0908 GMT - Malaysia's benchmark Kuala Lumpur Composite Index ended 0.7% higher at 1585.17. Market sentiment stayed positive as concerns of a full-scale trade war between the U.S. and China eased, alongside hopes for potential concession deals to delay or reduce tariffs, similarly to Mexico and Canada, TA Securities analyst Stephen Soo says in a note. Soo pegs the KLCI's support at 1550 and resistance at 1605. Among gainers, Petronas Gas was 2.5% higher and Gamuda Bhd. added 2.1%. Meanwhile, IOI Corp. was 1.1% lower and Mr. D.I.Y. Group lost 0.6%. (yingxian.wong@wsj.com)

0905 GMT - Crude futures find some support in early trade after Saudi Arabia lifted prices for March loadings of all crude grades it exports amid surging premiums for Middle Eastern oil. State-owned oil giant Aramco's flagship Arab Light crude to Asia, its main market, was raised by $2.40 a barrel to $3.90 a barrel over the Oman/Dubai benchmark--the largest monthly increase since August 2022. "This ties in with the strength that we have seen in the Middle East physical market since the start of the year," ING analysts say. Brent crude is up 0.1% at $74.71 a barrel, while WTI rises 0.3% to $71.22 a barrel. The benchmarks settled 2% lower in the previous trading session due to a larger-than-expected build in U.S. crude stockpiles and fears that a trade war between the U.S. and China could hurt global growth. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

data.title: Babcock International's Nuclear Division Has Been Underestimated — Market Talk
data.description: Investors have underestimated the potential of Babcock International's nuclear division, which is the standout performer in the U.K. defense company's latest update, JP Morgan analysts David Perry and Lucy Fitzgerald say. The U.K. government's commitment to the British nuclear-powered submarine fleet underpins the future growth of this division, they add. Some investors have raised concerns about the ability of the U.K. government to boost defense spending, but these seem unfounded, the analysts say. They point to a recent U.K. contract won by Rolls-Royce--its largest ever U.K. defense contract--as well as Babcock reiterating views of much stronger earnings from the U.K. defense market. Shares are up 11.5% at 605 pence. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Babcock International's Contract Backlog Not Expected to Grow Much — Market Talk
data.description: Babcock International hasn't disclosed its contract backlog, but Panmure analysts Joe Brent and Sanjay Vidyarthi say they don't expect it to grow by much. The U.K. defense company may benefit from global calls for increased defense spending, but should be wary of demands for better value for the taxpayer, which may result in more intense pressure on pricing, they warn. The analysts keep their hold recommendation but increase their target price on the stock to 550 pence from 490 pence. Shares are down 0.2% at 5.43 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: LVMH's Equity Story Unchanged, Berenberg Says; PT Up
data.description:
Berenberg on Thursday said LVMH's equity story remains unchanged even after a recent sell-off on the stock due to "unrealistic expectations" on its 2024 results.

As such, analysts raised their price target on the French stock to 740 euros from 720 euros with a Buy rating. The research firm also initiated coverage on the luxury giant's American Depositary Receipts at Buy with a price target of $154.

"With FX likely becoming a tailwind in FY25, balance sheet inventory provisions close to decade highs, and revenue growth set to resume, we model a recovery in gross and EBIT margins," the note said. "For FY26, we leave our EBIT and EPS estimates essentially unchanged, compared to a 1% drop in consensus."

data.title: Qualcomm Shares Slide Premarket as Investors Worry About External Factors — Market Talk
data.description: Qualcomm shares slip premarket as investors weigh a number of factors outside of the chip maker's control despite strong results for its fiscal first quarter, JPMorgan analysts write in a note to clients. Investors are concerned about the company losing business with Apple should the iPhone maker replace Qualcomm's chips in some phone models with in-house versions, the analysts say. Geopolitical headwinds, including the expiration of an agreement with Huawei, is also weighing on investors' minds, they say. Qualcomm stock closed 1.6% higher at $175.86 ahead of results on Wednesday. Shares are down 4% premarket, touching a low of $167.94. (mauro.orru@wsj.com)

data.title: Europe's Gas Price Rises on Colder Weather Forecast — Market Talk
data.description:
0931 GMT - European natural-gas prices rise in early trade on expectations of stronger demand due to a cold snap. "Forecasts for colder weather have raised concerns that we will see steeper draws in gas storage in the coming days," ING strategists Warren Patterson and Ewa Manthey say. EU inventories have been depleting faster than usual, with storage levels at 51.3% and daily net withdrawals of around 7.9 terawatt-hours as of Tuesday. The benchmark front-month contract at the Dutch TTF hub currently trades 1.3% higher to 54.13 euros a megawatt-hour and is up almost 15% on the month. Prices rebounded in the previous trading session after China said it would impose tariffs on U.S. LNG starting on Feb. 10, a move expected to free up larger volumes for other buyers, including Europe. (giulia.petroni@wsj.com)

0937 GMT - Sterling could fall modestly if the Bank of England votes 8-1 to cut interest rates and downgrades its economic growth forecasts, ING analyst Chris Turner says in a note. A 9-0 vote in favor of a cut would be much more negative for sterling, he says. The BOE announces its decision at 1200 GMT and is widely expected to cut rates by 25 basis points. The brief reprieve in the "tariff noise" surrounding President Trump's policies should allow investors to focus on the U.K.'s fiscal and monetary mix, Turner says. GBP/USD falls 0.5% to 1.2446. EUR/GBP rises 0.1% to 0.8328. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0931 GMT - Beijing may have to eventually stimulate domestic demand aggressively as it did during the previous trade war as China relies more on exports than the U.S. does, Macquarie analysts write in a note. The new tariffs released by China on U.S. goods cover only about $15 billion, while the new U.S. tariffs cover around $525 billion of Chinese goods, they note. Beijing could easily offset the impact of the new tariff with a CNY500 billion stimulus, Macquarie analysts say. China's measured response to Trump's new tariffs suggests its preference for negotiation over escalation, the analysts say, noting China's greater reliance on exports due to weak domestic demand. Beijing may also prefer to rely on exports to boost economic growth in order to save its policy tools for the future, they say. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0902 GMT - Taiwan's January exports growth likely moderated slightly to 8.7% on year, according to the median forecast of five estimates polled by The Wall Street Journal. That compares with a 9.2% gain in December following a 9.7% surge in November. The economists' estimates range from 3.6% to as high as 9.1%. While the Lunar New Year holiday falling earlier this year likely led to a sequential decline in monthly exports, HSBC thinks still-healthy structural demand for AI, along with some front-loading, likely helped mitigate the decline. DBS economists expect the island economy's exports to perform well in early 1Q, supported by front-loading of shipments ahead of potential U.S. tariffs. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0901 GMT - The Czech koruna rises after inflation data exceeded expectations ahead of the Czech National Bank's interest-rate decision at 1330 GMT. Czech annual inflation eased to 2.8% year-on-year in January from 3.0% in December but is above the 2.6% expected in a Reuters poll of analysts, according to LSEG. The CNB is likely to resume rate cuts with a 25 basis-point reduction, ING's Frantisek Taborsky says in a note. It will also unveil new forecasts that could support the case for further cuts, he says. However, CNB Governor Ales Michl will likely sound cautious. "Not much can be expected from EUR/CZK except higher volatility." EUR/CZK falls to 25.1457 after the data from 25.2166 beforehand. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0858 GMT - China's economic recovery has remained uneven, with persistent downward pressure on prices weighing on the outlook, says UOB economist Ho Woei Chen in a research note. China's travel and consumption demand over the Lunar New Year holiday has been improving since it lifted its Covid restrictions in late 2022, the economist notes. However, despite the encouraging data, PMI readings have indicated a smaller pace of services expansion in January, she notes. As the recently announced U.S. tariffs on China fell within expectations, UOB maintains its forecast for China's 2025 GDP growth at 4.3%. UOB is also closely monitoring developments on the tariff measures and the extent of China's fiscal policy boost. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0856 GMT - Yields on U.K. government bonds edge higher, reversing Wednesday's declines. Yields rise by less than those of eurozone bonds, however, as investors await a widely-expected interest-rate cut by the Bank of England at 1200 GMT. The BOE could signal readiness to cut interest rates faster than expected at future meetings due to the U.K.'s weak growth, RBC Capital Markets analysts say in a note. The 10-year gilt yield rises 1 basis point to 4.445%, while the German 10-year Bund yield rises 3 basis points to 2.386% Tradeweb data show. Markets price in a 95% probability of the BOE cutting interest rates by 25 basis points to 4.50%, LSEG Refinitiv data show. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

0835 GMT - The Bank of Japan should raise interest rates to at least above 1% if economic growth continues on greater flexibility in the job market and productivity improvements, says Masamichi Koike, deputy president and executive officer at Japanese megabank Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp. However, given that the country still hasn't completely overcome a deflationary mindset, "there may be a shock [to the economy] if the BOJ raises rates too rapidly," he says at an event in Tokyo. Meanwhile, Japan's corporate sector will likely witness more consolidations and bankruptcies this year as the economic recovery spurs healthy competition, he says. (megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com)

0802 GMT - Sterling falls as investors exercise caution ahead of the Bank of England's interest-rate decision at 1200 GMT. The market is pricing in a 94% chance of the BOE cutting rates by 25 basis points, according to LSEG. This marks the first decision since the U.K. government bond selloff last month. "Indeed, at the height of the pound's slump, there had been speculation about whether the BOE would cut at all at this meeting," Deutsche Bank analysts say in a note. However, the latest inflation data were lower than expected and this supported market bets for another cut. Deutsche Bank expects a 8-1 vote in favor of a cut. GBP/USD falls 0.2% to 1.2476. EUR/GBP rises 0.1% to 0.8325. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0753 GMT - The dollar edges higher as uncertainty around President Trump's trade policy remains elevated. Safe-haven demand should persist for the time being, with market participants seeking shelter in the dollar as a result of ongoing policy uncertainties, Pepperstone strategist Michael Brown says in a note. Trump's tariff plans will have a less detrimental impact on the U.S. economy compared to developed market peers, he says. This effectively puts the dollar in a position of being "the best of a bad bunch." The DXY dollar index rises 0.2% to 107.768 after hitting a one-week low of 107.296 Wednesday. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0722 GMT - The FTSE 100 is expected to open 73 points higher, or 0.9%, according to IG. The index closed 52 points higher, or 0.6%, to 8523 points Wednesday. Market sentiment across Europe and the U.S. has improved on waning trade tensions with President Trump, Swissquote Bank analyst Ipek Ozkardeskaya says in a note. Investors are also looking ahead to the latest policy decision from the Bank of England at 1200 GMT. The BOE is expected to lower interest rates by 25 basis points as U.K. Treasury chief Rachel Reeves's growth plans are "clouded by the country's restricted finances and the economy calls for BOE support," she says.(renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0714 GMT - The Philippine central bank is likely to cut its benchmark overnight reverse-repurchase rate by 25 bps to 5.50% next week, says HSBC. December and January CPI readings surprised on the upside but remains well within the lower-bound range of the BSP's 2%-4% target band, economist Aris Dacanay writes in a note. The central bank loosening its monetary policy will lend some much needed support to the economy after disappointing 4Q economic growth, he says. HSBC continues to expect the BSP to cut its policy rate by a total of 75 bps this year to 5.00%, while moving back its forecasts for the ending of the cycle to 4Q from 3Q. HSBC also projects the policy rate will remain constant at 5.00% throughout 2026. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Base Metals Rise on Weaker Dollar, Return of Chinese Buyers — Market Talk
data.description: LME three-month copper is up 0.6% at $9,325 a metric ton and LME three-month aluminum is up 0.4% at $2,633.50 a ton. Base metals prices have gained on a moderate risk-on sentiment, on a weaker U.S. dollar and the gradual return of Chinese participants to the market after the Lunar New Year holidays, Sucden Financial's Daria Efanova says. A continued increase in liquidity will further support the metals complex over the coming days, Efanova says in a note. Copper is firmly back above key resistance levels and is up 2.75% on week. Aluminum remains relatively subdued, up just 0.55% on week. That said, aluminum's spot price is higher than the main benchmark futures contract, indicating the market is fundamentally tight, Efanova adds. (joseph.hoppe@wsj.com)

data.title: Compass Group Outperforms Peers in a Challenging Environment — Market Talk
data.description: Compass Group is delivering well in an uncertain environment, Goodbody's Fintan Ryan writes in a note. The London-listed catering contractor showed a solid start to the fiscal year, outperforming its peers. In the first quarter, Compass Group reached 9.2% revenue growth, while Sodexo and Aramark achieved 4.6% and 5% organic sales growth, respectively, Ryan says. The Irish brokerage doesn't expect any change in consensus following the results. Compass Group and Sodexo shares are down 0.9% and 0.3% at 2,766 pence and 72.50 euros, respectively. (najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Salmon Evolution ASA: Invitation to presentation of Q4 results
data.description:
Salmon Evolution ASA hereby invites you to the presentation of the results for the fourth quarter 2024 at Hotel Continental in Oslo, Norway, Tuesday 11 February at 08:00 CET. A light snack with smoked salmon from Indre Harøy will be served from 07:45 CET.

The presentation and Q&A will be held in English, and participants may also follow the presentation and submit questions through a live webcast available on www.salmonevolution.no, or with the following link: https://events.webcast.no/salmon-evolution/OEpmPDaoyhi5siPjdg0b/itZLMjqYBJGd15MTcO2S

A recording of the webcast will be made available on our website shortly after the presentation.

Please note that the quarterly report and presentation will be published at 06:30 CET.
